lmp:Semantics.lmp:NeSyGrammar: MS{Ac}EDSSA[DOM:Protein kinase-like domain superfamily (InterPro)]LP[/DOM][DOM:STE20/SPS1-related Proline-Alanine-rich Kinase (InterPro)]WSINRDDYELQEV[BIND:ATP|PMID:14707132|ChEBI:ChEBI:CHEBI:30616]I[/BIND]GSGATAVVQAAYCAPKKEKVAI[BIND:ATP|PMID:14707132|ChEBI:ChEBI:CHEBI:30616]K[/BIND]RINLEKCQTSMDELLKEIQAMSQCHHPNIVSYYTSFVVKDELWLVMKLLSGGSVLDIIKHIVAKGEHKSGVLDESTIATILREVLEGLEYLHKNGQIHR[BIND:CAT]D[/BIND]VKAGNILLGEDGSVQIADFGVSAFLATGGDITRNKVRKT{P}FVGTPCWMAPEVMEQVRGYDFKADIWSFGITAIELATGAAPYHKYPPMKVLMLTLQNDPPSLETGVQDKEMLKKYGKSFRKMISLCLQKDPEKRPTAAELLRHKFFQKAKNKEFLQEKTLQRAPT{P}ISERAKKVRRVPGS{P}S{P}GRLHKTEDGGWEWS{P}DDEFDEES{P}EEGKAAISQLRS{P}PRVKESISNSELFPTTDPVGTLLQVPEQISAHLPQPAGQIATQPTQVSLPPTAEPAKTAQALSSGSGS{P}QETKIPISLVLRLRNSKKELNDIRFEFTPGRDTAEGVSQELISAGLVDGRDLVIVAANLQKIVEEPQSNRSVTFKLA[/DOM]SGVEGSDIPDDGKLIGFAQLSIS
lmp:Semantics.lmp:Comment: Effector serine/threonine-protein kinase component of the WNK-SPAK/OSR1 kinase cascade, which is involved in various processes, such as ion transport, response to hypertonic stress and blood pressure (PubMed:16669787, PubMed:18270262, PubMed:21321328, PubMed:34289367). Specifically recognizes and binds proteins with a RFXV motif (PubMed:16669787, PubMed:17721439, PubMed:21321328). Acts downstream of WNK kinases (WNK1, WNK2, WNK3 or WNK4): following activation by WNK kinases, catalyzes phosphorylation of ion cotransporters, such as SLC12A1/NKCC2, SLC12A2/NKCC1, SLC12A3/NCC, SLC12A5/KCC2 or SLC12A6/KCC3, regulating their activity (PubMed:17721439). Mediates regulatory volume increase in response to hyperosmotic stress by catalyzing phosphorylation of ion cotransporters SLC12A1/NKCC2, SLC12A2/NKCC1 and SLC12A6/KCC3 downstream of WNK1 and WNK3 kinases (PubMed:16669787, PubMed:21321328). Phosphorylation of Na-K-Cl cotransporters SLC12A2/NKCC1 and SLC12A2/NKCC1 promote their activation and ion influx; simultaneously, phosphorylation of K-Cl cotransporters SLC12A5/KCC2 and SLC12A6/KCC3 inhibit their activity, blocking ion efflux (PubMed:16669787, PubMed:19665974, PubMed:21321328). Acts as a regulator of NaCl reabsorption in the distal nephron by mediating phosphorylation and activation of the thiazide-sensitive Na-Cl cotransporter SLC12A3/NCC in distal convoluted tubule cells of kidney downstream of WNK4 (PubMed:18270262). Also acts as a regulator of angiogenesis in endothelial cells downstream of WNK1 (PubMed:23386621, PubMed:25362046). Acts as an activator of inward rectifier potassium channels KCNJ2/Kir2.1 and KCNJ4/Kir2.3 downstream of WNK1: recognizes and binds the RXFXV/I variant motif on KCNJ2/Kir2.1 and KCNJ4/Kir2.3 and regulates their localization to the cell membrane without mediating their phosphorylation (PubMed:29581290). Phosphorylates RELL1, RELL2 and RELT (PubMed:16389068, PubMed:28688764). Phosphorylates PAK1 (PubMed:14707132). Phosphorylates PLSCR1 in the presence of RELT (PubMed:22052202)
Activated following phosphorylation by WNK kinases (WNK1, WNK2, WNK3 or WNK4)
Ubiquitously expressed in all tissue examined

Phosphorylation at Thr-185 by WNK kinases (WNK1, WNK2, WNK3 or WNK4) is required for activation (PubMed:16669787, PubMed:16832045, PubMed:17190791, PubMed:21321328, PubMed:22989884, PubMed:29581290). Autophosphorylated; promoting its activity (PubMed:14707132, PubMed:22052202)
Belongs to the protein kinase superfamily. STE Ser/Thr protein kinase family. STE20 subfamily

lmp:Chain.sequence: MS{Ac}EDSSA[DOM:Protein kinase-like domain superfamily (InterPro)]LP[/DOM][DOM:STE20/SPS1-related Proline-Alanine-rich Kinase (InterPro)]WSINRDDYELQEV[BIND:ATP|PMID:14707132|ChEBI:ChEBI:CHEBI:30616]I[/BIND]GSGATAVVQAAYCAPKKEKVAI[BIND:ATP|PMID:14707132|ChEBI:ChEBI:CHEBI:30616]K[/BIND]RINLEKCQTSMDELLKEIQAMSQCHHPNIVSYYTSFVVKDELWLVMKLLSGGSVLDIIKHIVAKGEHKSGVLDESTIATILREVLEGLEYLHKNGQIHR[BIND:CAT]D[/BIND]VKAGNILLGEDGSVQIADFGVSAFLATGGDITRNKVRKT{P}FVGTPCWMAPEVMEQVRGYDFKADIWSFGITAIELATGAAPYHKYPPMKVLMLTLQNDPPSLETGVQDKEMLKKYGKSFRKMISLCLQKDPEKRPTAAELLRHKFFQKAKNKEFLQEKTLQRAPT{P}ISERAKKVRRVPGS{P}S{P}GRLHKTEDGGWEWS{P}DDEFDEES{P}EEGKAAISQLRS{P}PRVKESISNSELFPTTDPVGTLLQVPEQISAHLPQPAGQIATQPTQVSLPPTAEPAKTAQALSSGSGS{P}QETKIPISLVLRLRNSKKELNDIRFEFTPGRDTAEGVSQELISAGLVDGRDLVIVAANLQKIVEEPQSNRSVTFKLA[/DOM]SGVEGSDIPDDGKLIGFAQLSIS

lmp:CrossReference.lmp:Property: 2 sites, 1 N-linked glycan (1 site), 1 O-linked glycan (1 site)
7 GO annotations based on evolutionary models
Expressed in buccal mucosa cell and 209 other cell types or tissues
P:negative regulation of potassium ion transmembrane transport
P:positive regulation of T cell chemotaxis
Phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase Catalytic Subunit, Chain A, domain 1

lmp:Reference.lmp:Citation: Isolation and characterization of a novel serine threonine kinase gene on chromosome 3p22-21.3.
Prediction of the coding sequences of unidentified human genes. XIV. The complete sequences of 100 new cDNA clones from brain which code for large proteins in vitro.
The status, quality, and expansion of the NIH full-length cDNA project: the Mammalian Gene Collection (MGC).
Cloning and characterization of a novel human cDNA homologous to human DCHT mRNA.
Characterization of OSR1, a member of the mammalian Ste20p/germinal center kinase subfamily.
Identification of RELT homologues that associate with RELT and are phosphorylated by OSR1.
Functional interactions of the SPAK/OSR1 kinases with their upstream activator WNK1 and downstream substrate NKCC1.
WNK1 and OSR1 regulate the Na+, K+, 2Cl- cotransporter in HeLa cells.
Global, in vivo, and site-specific phosphorylation dynamics in signaling networks.
Regulation of activity and localization of the WNK1 protein kinase by hyperosmotic stress.
ATM and ATR substrate analysis reveals extensive protein networks responsive to DNA damage.
Activation of the thiazide-sensitive Na+-Cl- cotransporter by the WNK-regulated kinases SPAK and OSR1.
A quantitative atlas of mitotic phosphorylation.
Large-scale phosphoproteome analysis of human liver tissue by enrichment and fractionation of phosphopeptides with strong anion exchange chromatography.
Lys-N and trypsin cover complementary parts of the phosphoproteome in a refined SCX-based approach.
Sites of regulated phosphorylation that control K-Cl cotransporter activity.
Quantitative phosphoproteomic analysis of T cell receptor signaling reveals system-wide modulation of protein-protein interactions.
Quantitative phosphoproteomics reveals widespread full phosphorylation site occupancy during mitosis.
Initial characterization of the human central proteome.
Regulation of the NKCC2 ion cotransporter by SPAK-OSR1-dependent and -independent pathways.
System-wide temporal characterization of the proteome and phosphoproteome of human embryonic stem cell differentiation.
Interactions with WNK (with no lysine) family members regulate oxidative stress response 1 and ion co-transporter activity.
Systematic validation of antibody binding and protein subcellular localization using siRNA and confocal microscopy.
Identification of PLSCR1 as a protein that interacts with RELT family members.
Comparative large-scale characterisation of plant vs. mammal proteins reveals similar and idiosyncratic N-alpha acetylation features.
Toward a comprehensive characterization of a human cancer cell phosphoproteome.
WNK1 protein kinase regulates embryonic cardiovascular development through the OSR1 signaling cascade.
An enzyme assisted RP-RPLC approach for in-depth analysis of human liver phosphoproteome.
Actions of the protein kinase WNK1 on endothelial cells are differentially mediated by its substrate kinases OSR1 and SPAK.
RELT family members activate p38 and induce apoptosis by a mechanism distinct from TNFR1.
OSR1 regulates a subset of inward rectifier potassium channels via a binding motif variant.
Phosphorylated WNK kinase networks in recoded bacteria recapitulate physiological function.
Structural insights into the recognition of substrates and activators by the OSR1 kinase.
Patterns of somatic mutation in human cancer genomes.
